sentences.sentence: There is no doubt that dogs are the oldest of all species tamed by humans and their domestication was based on a mutually beneficial relationship with man.
sentences.translation: 毫无疑问，狗是人类驯化的所有物种中最早的一种，而其驯化建立在人与狗互利的关系之上。

structure.text: that dogs are the oldest of all species tamed by humans
and their domestication was based on a mutually beneficial relationship with man

difficult_points: that 从句说明 doubt 的具体内容；tamed by humans 修饰 species。
and 连接 that 从句内部的两个判断，their 指 dogs。

sentences.sentence: The conventional view is that the domestication of wolves began between 10,000 and 20,000 years ago.
sentences.translation: 传统观点认为，狼的驯化始于1万至2万年前。

structure.text: that the domestication of wolves began between 10,000 and 20,000 years ago

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明 conventional view 的内容。
between ... and ... 表示时间范围。

sentences.sentence: However, a recent ground-breaking paper by a group of international geneticists has pushed this date back by a factor of 10.
sentences.translation: 然而，一组国际遗传学家最近发表的一篇突破性论文，把这一时间向前推了十倍。

structure.text: a recent ground-breaking paper by a group of international geneticists

difficult_points: this date 回指传统观点中的驯化起始时间。
push a date back 表示把时间追溯得更早；by a factor of 10 表示扩大十倍。

sentences.sentence: Led by Dr. Robert Wayne, at the University of California, Los Angeles, the team showed that all dog breeds had only one ancestor, the wolf.
sentences.translation: 该团队由加州大学洛杉矶分校的罗伯特·韦恩博士带领，研究表明所有犬种只有一个祖先——狼。

structure.text: Led by Dr. Robert Wayne, at the University of California, Los Angeles
that all dog breeds had only one ancestor

difficult_points: Led by ... 的逻辑主语是 the team，说明团队领导者。
that 从句作 showed 的宾语；the wolf 与 one ancestor 同位。

sentences.sentence: They did this by analysing the genetic history through the DINA of 162 wolves from around the world and 140 domestic dogs representing 67 breeds.
sentences.translation: 他们通过分析来自世界各地的162只狼和代表67个品种的140只家犬的“DINA”，研究其遗传历史，从而得出这一结论。

structure.text: through the DINA of 162 wolves from around the world and 140 domestic dogs representing 67 breeds

difficult_points: this 回指证明所有犬种拥有共同狼祖先；by + 动名词说明研究手段。
representing ... 修饰 140 domestic dogs。原文 DINA 很可能是 DNA 的拼写错误，必须原样保留。

sentences.sentence: The research also confirms, for the first time, that dogs are descended only from wolves and do not share DNA with coyotes or jackals.
sentences.translation: 该研究还首次证实，狗只源自狼，与郊狼或胡狼并不共享DNA。

structure.text: that dogs are descended only from wolves and do not share DNA with coyotes or jackals

difficult_points: that 从句作 confirms 的宾语，内部有两个并列谓语。
be descended from 表示物种源自某祖先；only 限定 wolves。

sentences.sentence: The fact that our companionship with dogs now appears to go back at least 100,000 years means that this partnership may have played an important part in the development of human hunting techniques that developed 70,000 to 90,000 years ago.
sentences.translation: 我们与狗的伙伴关系如今看来至少可追溯到10万年前；这一事实意味着，这种伙伴关系可能在人类7万至9万年前发展起来的狩猎技术中发挥了重要作用。

structure.text: that our companionship with dogs now appears to go back at least 100,000 years
that this partnership may have played an important part in the development of human hunting techniques

difficult_points: 第一个 that 从句说明 fact 内容，第二个 that 从句作 means 的宾语。
第三个 that 定语从句修饰 hunting techniques；may have played 表示对过去作用的推测。

sentences.sentence: It also may even have affected the brain development in both species.
sentences.translation: 这种伙伴关系甚至还可能影响了两个物种的大脑发育。

structure.text: the brain development in both species

difficult_points: It 回指人类与狗的长期伙伴关系。
may have affected 表示对过去影响的推测；also 与 even 共同加强语气。

sentences.sentence: The Australian veterinarian David Paxton suggests that in that period of first contact, people did not so much domesticate wolves as wolves domesticated people.
sentences.translation: 澳大利亚兽医大卫·帕克斯顿认为，在双方最初接触的时期，与其说是人类驯化了狼，不如说是狼驯化了人类。

structure.text: The Australian veterinarian David Paxton suggests
that in that period of first contact
people did not so much domesticate wolves as wolves domesticated people

difficult_points: that 从句作 suggests 的宾语。
not so much A as B 是固定对比结构，意为“与其说A，不如说B”。

expressions.text: in that period of first contact

sentences.sentence: Wolves may have started living at the edge of human settlements as scavengers, eating scraps of food and waste.
sentences.translation: 狼可能开始以食腐动物的身份生活在人类定居点边缘，吃食物残渣和废弃物。

structure.text: at the edge of human settlements
eating scraps of food and waste

difficult_points: may have started 表示对过去起始行为的推测，start 后接动名词 living。
eating ... 补充说明狼作为 scavengers 的行为。

sentences.sentence: Some learned to live with human beings in a mutually helpful way and gradually evolved into dogs.
sentences.translation: 其中一些狼学会了以互助方式与人类共同生活，并逐渐进化成狗。

structure.text: learned to live with human beings

difficult_points: Some 指部分狼，而不是人类。
learned 与 evolved 是并列谓语；evolve into 表示逐渐进化成。

sentences.sentence: At the very least, they would have protected human settlements, and given warnings by barking at anything approaching.
sentences.translation: 至少，它们会保护人类定居点，并通过对任何靠近的事物吠叫来发出警报。

structure.text: they would have protected human settlements

difficult_points: would have protected/given 表示对早期狼可能作用的推断，第二个 would have 被省略。
by + 动名词说明示警方式；approaching 修饰 anything。

sentences.sentence: The wolves that evolved into dogs have been enormously successful in evolutionary terms.
sentences.translation: 从进化角度看，那些进化成狗的狼取得了巨大的成功。
difficult_points: that 从句修饰 wolves。
in ... terms 表示从某一角度衡量，成功指后代繁盛和广泛分布。

sentences.sentence: They are found everywhere in the inhabited world, hundreds of millions of them.
sentences.translation: 在有人居住的世界各地都能见到它们，数量达到数亿只。

structure.text: are found everywhere in the inhabited world

difficult_points: They 指狗；are found 表示分布于、可见于。
hundreds of millions of them 是补充数量说明，属于省略式附加成分。

sentences.sentence: The descendants of the wolves that remained wolves are now sparsely distributed, often in endangered populations.
sentences.translation: 而那些仍保持狼形态的狼之后代，如今分布稀疏，往往属于濒危种群。
difficult_points: that 从句修饰 wolves，remained 为系动词，wolves 作表语。
sparsely distributed 与前句狗遍布世界形成对比。

sentences.sentence: In return for companionship and food, the early ancestor of the dog assisted humans in tracking, hunting, guarding and a variety of other activities.
sentences.translation: 作为陪伴和食物的回报，狗的早期祖先协助人类进行追踪、狩猎、守卫以及其他多种活动。

structure.text: In return for companionship and food
the early ancestor of the dog
in tracking, hunting, guarding and a variety of other activities

difficult_points: In return for 表示交换关系。
assist somebody in doing 表示协助某人做事，三个动名词并列。

sentences.sentence: Eventually humans began to selectively breed these animals for specific traits.
sentences.translation: 最终，人类开始为了获得特定性状而对这些动物进行选择性繁育。

structure.text: began to selectively breed these animals

difficult_points: selectively 修饰 breed，表示按需要选择个体繁育。
for specific traits 说明选择性繁育所追求的特征。

sentences.sentence: Physical characteristics changed and individual breeds began to take shape.
sentences.translation: 狗的身体特征发生变化，不同品种也开始逐渐成形。

structure.text: and individual breeds began to take shape

difficult_points: and 连接两个完整分句，描述选择性繁育的两项结果。
take shape 是固定表达，表示逐渐形成。

sentences.sentence: As humans wandered across Asia and Europe, they took their dogs along, using them for additional tasks and further breeding them for selected qualities that would better enable them to perform specific duties.
sentences.translation: 随着人类迁徙穿越亚洲和欧洲，他们也带着狗同行，让狗承担更多任务，并进一步按照选定品质进行繁育，使它们更能胜任特定职责。

structure.text: As humans wandered across Asia and Europe
and further breeding them for selected qualities
that would better enable them to perform specific duties

difficult_points: As 从句说明人类迁徙过程；using 与 breeding 是并列分词短语，逻辑主语为 humans。
that 从句修饰 selected qualities，enable + 宾语 + to do 表示使狗能履行职责。

sentences.sentence: According to Dr. Colin Groves, of the Department of Archaeology and Anthropology at Australian National University, early humans came to rely on dogs' keen ability to hear, smell and see - allowing certain areas of the human brain to shrink in size relative to oilier areas.'Dogs acted as human's alarm systems, trackers and hunting aids, garbage disposal facilities, hot-water bottles and children's guardians and playmates.
sentences.translation: 据科林·格罗夫斯博士所说，早期人类逐渐依赖狗敏锐的听觉、嗅觉和视觉能力，从而使人脑某些区域相对于其他区域缩小。“狗充当人类的警报系统、追踪者、狩猎助手、垃圾处理设施、热水袋，以及儿童的守护者和玩伴。

structure.text: According to Dr. Colin Groves, of the Department of Archaeology and Anthropology at Australian National University
early humans came to rely on dogs' keen ability to hear, smell and see
allowing certain areas of the human brain to shrink in size relative to oilier areas
human's alarm systems, trackers and hunting aids, garbage disposal facilities, hot-water bottles and children's guardians and playmates

difficult_points: come to rely on 表示逐渐开始依赖；hear、smell、see 是 ability 后并列不定式。
allowing ... 是结果分词短语；原文 oilier areas 很可能是 other areas 的误写。
原文在 areas. 与 'Dogs 之间没有空格，因此两部分必须作为同一个句子块保留。
act as 后接一长串并列表语；human's 的单数所有格也按源文保留。

sentences.sentence: Humans provided dogs with food and security.
sentences.translation: 人类则为狗提供食物和安全保障。
difficult_points: provide somebody with something 表示为某人或动物提供某物。
本句与前句狗为人类提供服务构成互惠关系。

expressions.meaning_zh: 为……提供食物和安全保障

sentences.sentence: This symbiotic relationship was stable for over 100,000 years and intensified into mutual domestication,' said Dr. Groves.
sentences.translation: 格罗夫斯博士说：“这种共生关系稳定持续了十万多年，并逐渐加强为相互驯化。”
difficult_points: 主句是直接引语的后半部分，句末 said Dr. Groves 为倒装报道语。
was stable 与 intensified 是并列谓语；原文在 domestication 后用逗号加单引号闭合前文引语，需保留。

sentences.sentence: In his opinion, humans domesticated dogs and dogs domesticated humans.
sentences.translation: 他认为，人类驯化了狗，狗也驯化了人类。
difficult_points: and 连接两个结构完全对称的分句，突出相互驯化。
In his opinion 明确该说法是 Groves 的观点。

sentences.sentence: Dr. Groves repealed an assertion made as early as 1914 that humans have some of the same physical characteristics as domesticated animals, the most notable being decreased brain size.
sentences.translation: 格罗夫斯博士重申了一项早在1914年就提出的论断：人类具有一些与驯养动物相同的身体特征，其中最显著的是脑容量减小。

structure.text: that humans have some of the same physical characteristics as domesticated animals
the most notable being decreased brain size

difficult_points: made ... 修饰 assertion，that 从句说明论断内容。
the most notable being ... 是独立主格，补充最显著特征。
原文 repealed 通常意为“废除”，结合语境很可能是 repeated（重申）的误写，必须原样保留。

sentences.sentence: The horse experienced a 16 percent reduction in brain size after domestication while pigs'brains shrank by as much as 34 percent.
sentences.translation: 马在驯化后脑容量减少了16%，而猪的脑容量缩小幅度高达34%。

structure.text: The horse experienced a 16 percent reduction in brain size
while pigs'brains shrank by as much as 34 percent

difficult_points: while 对比马与猪脑容量缩小的幅度。
by 表示变化幅度；as much as 强调高达34%。原文 pigs'brains 缺少空格，须保留。

sentences.sentence: The estimated brain-size reduction in domesticated dogs varies from 30 percent to 10 percent.
sentences.translation: 据估计，家犬脑容量的缩减幅度在10%至30%之间。

structure.text: The estimated brain-size reduction in domesticated dogs

difficult_points: estimated 是过去分词形容词，表示数据为估算值。
原文范围按 30 percent to 10 percent 的降序书写，需原样保留，语义仍是10%至30%的区间。

sentences.sentence: Only in the last decade have archaeologists uncovered enough fossil evidence to establish that brain capacity in humans declined in Europe and Africa by at least 10 percent beginning about 10,000 years ago.
sentences.translation: 直到最近十年，考古学家才发现足够的化石证据，证实约从1万年前开始，欧洲和非洲人类的脑容量至少下降了10%。

structure.text: have archaeologists uncovered enough fossil evidence
that brain capacity in humans declined in Europe and Africa by at least 10 percent beginning about 10,000 years ago

difficult_points: Only + 时间状语置于句首引起部分倒装，助动词 have 提到主语前。
to establish 表示发现证据的用途，that 从句作 establish 的宾语。
beginning ... 修饰脑容量下降的起始时间。

sentences.sentence: Dr. Groves believes this reduction may have taken place as the relationship between humans and dogs intensified.
sentences.translation: 格罗夫斯博士认为，这种缩减可能发生在人类与狗的关系不断加强之时。

structure.text: this reduction may have taken place
as the relationship between humans and dogs intensified

difficult_points: believes 后省略 that，引出宾语从句。
may have taken place 表示对过去的推测；as 引导时间从句，表示关系加强过程中。

sentences.sentence: The close interaction between the two species allowed for the diminishing of certain human brain functions like smell and hearing.
sentences.translation: 两个物种之间的密切互动，使人类嗅觉和听觉等某些大脑功能得以减弱。

structure.text: The close interaction between the two species
allowed for the diminishing of certain human brain functions

difficult_points: allow for 在此表示使某种结果成为可能；the diminishing 是名词化结构。
like smell and hearing 举例说明 certain human brain functions。
